visa_types.overview:
Overview
Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category, kindly refer to the information sheet of documents required.
Applicants applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear in person at the visa application center to register their biometrics which includes scans of all fingerprints and live digital photo.
In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.
Important message regarding legalization when applying for a Long Stay visa for Belgium:

Requests for the legalizations of documents will not be accepted if they are submitted together with the visa application. It is the responsibility of the applicant to present a compete visa file, and this includes all necessary documents already duly legalized. Applicants must ensure that all required documents are legalized before booking a visa appointment. Introducing an incomplete file may lead to a refusal, and it is not possible to add extra documents to your application at a later stage at your initiative, unless specifically requested by the Embassy.

How to legalize your document before submitting a visa application?
Via the Embassy: by appointment of via courier service. Please check the procedure: Documents and certificates issued in the UAE | FPS Foreign Affairs - Foreign Trade and Development Cooperation

How to add the legalized documents in your visa application?
Make sure to add a printed version of the proof of legalization together with the document in question. If you received a QR code to prove the different legalizations, it is not sufficient to only add this QR code to your file. You must consult the QR code yourself and print the necessary proofs of legalized documents.

Overview
Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category, kindly refer to the information sheet of documents required
            Please click on Do I need a visa? (https://immigration-portal.ec.europa.eu/do-i-need-visa_en) to find  out whether a Schengen visa is required or check on the interactive map (https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/policies/schengen-borders-and-visa/schengen-area_en) to learn  more about the Schengen  area.
Guarantees for return (assessment of migration risk): The applicant is requested to provide as much information as possible to document his/her family background, professional and socio-economic status, ownership of land or real estate (for instance: proof of family bonds with his/her country of origin, proof of regular income and stable source of revenues from the applicant and/or his/her partner, proof of property, etc.)
This information is essential to correctly judge the intention of the applicant to leave the Schengen area before the expiry of his/her visa.
In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below:

General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain light-coloured background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes. Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
- Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note: If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
The processing time for the application at the Embassy of Belgium is approximately 2 to 6 weeks.
Please be advised that due to the above mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel. 
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences

## Download Form
Please click here (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/) to fill the online Visa form

## Photo Specifications
Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below:
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain light-coloured background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes. Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
- Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note: If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
 Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category, kindly refer to the information sheet of documents required.
Applicants applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear in person at the visa application center to register their biometrics which includes scans of all fingerprints and live digital photo.
In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.
Important message regarding legalization when applying for a Long Stay visa for Belgium:

Requests for the legalizations of documents will not be accepted if they are submitted together with the visa application. It is the responsibility of the applicant to present a compete visa file, and this includes all necessary documents already duly legalized. Applicants must ensure that all required documents are legalized before booking a visa appointment. Introducing an incomplete file may lead to a refusal, and it is not possible to add extra documents to your application at a later stage at your initiative, unless specifically requested by the Embassy.

How to legalize your document before submitting a visa application?
Via the Embassy: by appointment of via courier service. Please check the procedure: Documents and certificates issued in the UAE | FPS Foreign Affairs - Foreign Trade and Development Cooperation

How to add the legalized documents in your visa application?
Make sure to add a printed version of the proof of legalization together with the document in question. If you received a QR code to prove the different legalizations, it is not sufficient to only add this QR code to your file. You must consult the QR code yourself and print the necessary proofs of legalized documents.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category, kindly refer to the information sheet of documents required.
Please click on Do I need a visa? (https://immigration-portal.ec.europa.eu/do-i-need-visa_en) to find out whether a Schengen visa is required or check on the interactive map (https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/policies/schengen-borders-and-visa/schengen-area_en) to learn more about the Schengen area.
Guarantees for return (assessment of migration risk): The applicant is requested to provide as much information as possible to document his/her family background, professional and socio-economic status, ownership of land or real estate (for instance: proof of family bonds with his/her country of origin, proof of regular income and stable source of revenues from the applicant and/or his/her partner, proof of property, etc.)
This information is essential to correctly judge the intention of the applicant to leave the Schengen area before the expiry of his/her visa.
In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.

## Visa Fees

[bel > are > en > Table:NANNIES > Visa Fees 1]

Please note:
- Please be informed that the fees charged by the Embassy or VFS Global are based on the current exchange rate in local currency and may be subject to change without prior notification
- Applicant shall be levied a service charge of AED 130.50 per application over and above visa fees (all applicable taxes included).

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, Please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/are/en/bel/travel-insurance)

Please click here for the checklist for Cultural and Sport Events

## Photo Specifications
Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below:
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain light-coloured background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes. Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
- Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note: If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
The processing time for the application at the Embassy of Belgium is approximately 2 to 6 weeks.
Please be advised that due to the above mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel. 
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences

## Download Form
Please click here (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/) to fill the online Visa form.

Important message regarding legalization when applying for a Long Stay visa for Belgium:

Requests for the legalizations of documents will not be accepted if they are submitted together with the visa application. It is the responsibility of the applicant to present a compete visa file, and this includes all necessary documents already duly legalized. Applicants must ensure that all required documents are legalized before booking a visa appointment. Introducing an incomplete file may lead to a refusal, and it is not possible to add extra documents to your application at a later stage at your initiative, unless specifically requested by the Embassy.

How to legalize your document before submitting a visa application?
Via the Embassy: by appointment of via courier service. Please check the procedure: Documents and certificates issued in the UAE | FPS Foreign Affairs - Foreign Trade and Development Cooperation

How to add the legalized documents in your visa application?
Make sure to add a printed version of the proof of legalization together with the document in question. If you received a QR code to prove the different legalizations, it is not sufficient to only add this QR code to your file. You must consult the QR code yourself and print the necessary proofs of legalized documents.

Guarantees for return (assessment of migration risk): The applicant is requested to provide as much information as possible to document his/her family background, professional and socio-economic status, ownership of land or real estate (for instance: proof of family bonds with his/her country of origin, proof of regular income and stable source of revenues from the applicant and/or his/her partner, proof of property, etc.)
This information is essential to correctly judge the intention of the applicant to leave the Schengen area before the expiry of his/her visa.
In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below:
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain light-coloured background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes. Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
- Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note: If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

Please be advised that due to the above mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel. 
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences

If you received a QR code to prove the different legalizations, it is not sufficient to only add this QR code to your file. You must consult the QR code yourself and print the necessary proofs of legalized documents.

Requests for the legalizations of documents will not be accepted if they are submitted together with the visa application. It is the responsibility of the applicant to present a compete visa file, and this includes all necessary documents already duly legalized. Applicants must ensure that all required documents are legalized before booking a visa appointment. Introducing an incomplete file may lead to a refusal, and it is not possible to add extra documents to your application at a later stage at your initiative, unless specifically requested by the Embassy.

How to legalize your document before submitting a visa application?
Via the Embassy: by appointment of via courier service. Please check the procedure: Documents and certificates issued in the UAE | FPS Foreign Affairs - Foreign Trade and Development Cooperation

How to add the legalized documents in your visa application?
Make sure to add a printed version of the proof of legalization together with the document in question. If you received a QR code to prove the different legalizations, it is not sufficient to only add this QR code to your file. You must consult the QR code yourself and print the necessary proofs of legalized documents.
